evaluation.manager_summary: Exemplary crisis intake call. Katherine presented with active alcohol withdrawal including hallucinations; Deborah immediately recognized the medical urgency, triaged to same-day detox admission, ensured safe transport, captured insurance, and stayed on the line until the caller was arranging her Uber. Textbook urgency handling with strong empathy and compliance throughout.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent immediately acknowledged Katherine's distress by name, validated her fear, and matched the urgency of the situation without being alarmist. The agent maintained a supportive, present tone throughout and checked in on her emotional state ('How are you feeling right now').

Agent captured carrier, member ID, and group number efficiently without disrupting the urgency-focused flow. Given the crisis nature of the call, the streamlined approach was appropriate, though no explicit verification step was mentioned.

Agent effectively screened for critical withdrawal indicators (seizure history, hallucinations) and captured substance use context (alcohol, attempting to stop). However, prior treatment history and frequency/duration of drinking were not explored, though this is reasonable given the crisis nature.

Agent immediately recognized severe withdrawal symptoms (shaking, vomiting, auditory hallucinations) as medically urgent, correctly escalated to same-day admission, assessed safety (alone, driving capability), and arranged immediate transport. Crisis response was textbook.
Caller is the patient herself (Katherine, speaking about her own symptoms). This dimension does not apply.

When Katherine said 'I think' she could drive (suggesting hesitation/objection), the agent immediately addressed the safety concern with a clear alternative (rideshare) without being dismissive. The agent acknowledged and redirected appropriately.

Agent provided crystal-clear next steps: call Uber immediately, specific address (repeated back for confirmation), what to say at arrival, and a contingency plan if symptoms worsen. Ownership and timing were explicit (today, detox team expecting her).

Agent used appropriate intake-level language ('withdrawal symptoms,' 'medically serious,' 'needs medical supervision') without diagnosing or making treatment guarantees. Stayed clearly within scope by describing symptoms and need for medical oversight without clinical assessment.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Katherine, I hear you and I'm glad you called. What you're describing — the shaking, the vomiting, two days in — those are withdrawal symptoms

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: How are you feeling right now, in this moment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Good. Relief is the right feeling. You did the right thing calling. We'll take it from here.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Do you have health insurance?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, Meridian Care. Member ID BP12856650, group GRP-769687.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I have that. I'm putting a note in right now with your name, insurance, and that you're coming in today.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Have you had any seizures before when you've tried to stop drinking, or any hallucinations — seeing or hearing things that aren't there?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: No seizures. But last night I thought I heard someone in the apartment and there was nobody there.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: What you're describing — the shaking, the vomiting, two days in — those are withdrawal symptoms

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Katherine, that's an early sign of alcohol withdrawal that we take very seriously. I want to be direct with you: alcohol withdrawal at this stage can escalate

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: it needs medical supervision. I want to get you into our detox unit today. Can you get here, or do you need us to help arrange transport?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: call us back if anything gets worse before you arrive — new symptoms, chest pain, confusion.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I can drive. I think.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'd actually recommend not driving given what you're describing — your coordination may be affected. Do you have someone who could bring you, or can you take a rideshare?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I can call an Uber.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Here's what I need from you: call the Uber right after we hang up. Our address is 4800 North Lakeview Drive. When you arrive, tell the front desk your name

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm putting a note in right now with your name, insurance, and that you're coming in today. Our detox team will be expecting you.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: 4800 North Lakeview Drive.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: What you're describing — the shaking, the vomiting, two days in — those are withdrawal symptoms and they can be medically serious.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: that's an early sign of alcohol withdrawal that we take very seriously... it needs medical supervision.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: alcohol withdrawal at this stage can escalate
